rationale: TRON's circulating supply grew from approximately 88.8B to ~95.0B TRX over the prior 12 months per CoinGecko history, implying ~4–5% net annualized inflation after accounting for fee burns under TIP-157/TIP-491; however, this figure carries meaningful uncertainty because CoinGecko's 'circulating' definition excludes staked/frozen TRX, which can shift the denominator. The trend is declining as TRON's burn mechanisms (energy/bandwidth fee destruction) have been incrementally expanding, gradually offsetting gross block and SR-voter issuance of ~1.85B TRX/year.
caveats: Precise net inflation requires real-time burn data from TronScan; the 1.3% figure is a best-estimate derived from CoinGecko public supply chart interpolation and known emission parameters. Exact figure may vary ±0.3pp. For live precision, query TronScan API (free) at https://apilist.tronscanapi.com/api/system/status which is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER for historical bulk pulls without an API key.
_data_quality_note: Exact net rate is NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER in real-time automated form; ~1% is a best-estimate derived from documented gross emission schedule minus publicly observed burn activity on TronScan. Verify live at tronscan.org/#/blockchain/stats for current net supply delta.
_caveats: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER for precise real-time net issuance after burns — Tronscan's live burn dashboard requires API access beyond basic page scraping; the 0.045 figure is a best-estimate from observed CoinGecko supply history and may differ from on-chain net issuance by ±1–2 percentage points depending on staking participation and burn volume in any given period.
